scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a production-accounting-specific posting summary tied to budget coding, timecards, Exhibit G reports, payroll, AP support, and ledger evidence.
Missing production type, signatory, incentive, and system context triggered a blocked-on-prerequisites response instead of a generic accounting answer.
Unsupported payroll or AP changes were refused because the underlying timecard, invoice, or approval trail did not support the requested adjustment.
Missing call sheet, DPR, timecard, invoice, or petty-cash support kept the item blocked and preserved the evidence gap explicitly.
The lane rejected an unsupported reclassification request and preserved the original evidence trail for escalation.
Cost-ledger, payroll, petty-cash, AP, and bank mismatches were treated as reconciliation work and not flattened into an unsupported correction.
Qualified-spend and incentive-support gaps were escalated with the unresolved production-accounting evidence package intact.
Controller, legal, labor-relations, payroll-tax, and certification asks were refused and routed to adjacent owners.
The response used realistic production-accounting terminology such as hot cost, Exhibit G, DPR, fringe detail, petty cash, and final cost report support.
The pack refused to treat the industry label as cosmetic and required actual production model, signatory, and incentive context.
Conflicting public guidance, signatory obligations, and tenant policy produced escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.
Broadcast and scripted production paths stayed separate, with assumptions and next owners made explicit instead of blended into one process.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to production paperwork, payroll and fringe support, petty-cash and AP support, bank and cost-report reconciliation, and incentive-support handling.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all revenue-billing-and-financial-reconciliation abilities, and the cross-industry ability required by the issue.
